response.step_by_step_log: I first identified the manga-style dialogue at the start of the video. The 3D performance starting at 00:43 clearly displayed the song title 'きゅびずむ' and composer '原口沙輔'. I performed a search for 'きゅびずむ 原口沙輔 Japanese and English lyrics' and successfully retrieved the song's lyrics. The video ends with further comic-style dialogue, which I noted.
response.global_summary: The video opens with a comic-style dialogue between two characters (Ninomae Ina'nis and Pavolia Reine). This transitions into a 3D musical performance of the song 'Kyubijizumu' by Sasuke Haraguchi, performed by the two characters. The video then concludes with another comic-style dialogue involving the same characters and a third character (Takanashi Kiara).

scenes.description: Characters Ninomae Ina'nis and Pavolia Reine are discussing a crime scene in a manga-style comic panel.
A 3D animated musical performance by Ninomae Ina'nis and Pavolia Reine, performing the song 'Kyubijizumu'. The title and credits appear on-screen.
scenes.reference_lyrics_og:
大きな白いキャンバスに 虚構のコラージュを貼るよ
誇張された天使が居たよ
薔薇 人 歪んだ 正しくなくても見えた
生きるため立ち寄った美術館のセザンヌに
なんもわかんないな
まだ知らずにいても描けた 赤
scenes.reference_lyrics_en:
I'm going to make a collage of fiction, and put it up on a big white canvas.
There was an exaggerated angel.
Roses, people, distorted, even though it didn't look right, it was visible.
To live, I stopped by Cezanne at the art museum.
I don't understand anything at all.
Even without knowing, I could draw it, red.

scenes.description: The video returns to manga-style comic panels, showing a dialogue between Ninomae Ina'nis, Pavolia Reine, and Takanashi Kiara regarding the investigation.
